1.1: Paul a prisoner of Yeshua The Messiah, and brother Timotheus, to beloved Philemon and our companion laborer,
1.2: And to Aphia our beloved and to Arkippus our fellow laborer and to the church that is in your house.
1.3: Grace be with you and peace from God Our Father and from our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.
1.4: I thank my God always, and I remember you in my prayers,
1.5: Behold, from the time that I heard of your faith and love which you have toward our Lord Yeshua and toward all The Holy Ones,
1.6: That the sharing of your faith would produce fruit in works and in the knowledge of every good thing which you have in Yeshua The Messiah.
1.7: For we have great joy and comfort, for by your love the affections of the Saints have been refreshed.
1.8: Because of this, I have great freedom in The Messiah to command you those things that are right.
1.9: But for love's sake, I beg of you, I Paul, who am The Elder, as you know, and also a prisoner of Yeshua The Messiah,
1.10: I beg of you concerning my son Onesimus, whom I begot in my bondage,
1.11: He who was not useful to you at times, but now also is very useful to you and to me,
1.12: And I sent him to you, so receive him as my son.
1.13: For I was willing to keep him with me to minister to me on your behalf in the bondage of The Gospel.
1.14: But I did not want to do anything without your counsel, so that your benefit would not be by necessity, but by your will.
1.15: But perhaps also for this cause he departed for a time, so that you may have him for eternity,
1.16: Not as a Servant from now on, but more than a Servant; as my particularly beloved brother, how much more so yours, both in the flesh and in Our Lord?
1.17: If, therefore, you are my partner, receive him as mine.
1.18: And if you lack anything or he owes you a debt, put it to my account.
1.19: I, Paul, have written with my hand; I myself will pay, without saying to you also that you owe me your soul.
1.20: Yes, my brother, I am refreshed by you in Our Lord. Satisfy my love in The Messiah.
1.21: Because I trust that you will listen to me, I have written to you and I know that you will do more than what I have said.
1.22: But prepare for me at once a dwelling place, for I hope soon to be given to you by your prayers.
1.23: Epaphra, a fellow captive in Yeshua The Messiah, invokes your peace,
1.24: And Marcus, Aristarchus, Dema and Luqa, my helpers.
1.25: The grace of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah be with your spirit. Amen.
